title: baileya multiradiata var. perennis
intro: Baileya multiradiata var. perennis, also known as the desert marigold or Mexican gold poppy, is a perennial variety of the Baileya multiradiata plant, native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Baileya multiradiata var. perennis include its ability to protect against oxidative stress, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
